channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Iran Economic Collapse May Come Too Late for Trump
item.description: Weeks of conflict have exacerbated Iran economic woes, but the Islamic Republic appears able to survive a Gulf standoff despite a US blockade cutting off energy exports.

item.title: US-Iran War Deadline Passes as 60-Day War Powers Mark Arrives
item.description: The May 1 deadline under the War Powers Resolution has arrived with no resolution in sight for the US-Iran conflict, as the administration faces questions about congressional authorization.

item.title: ECB Rate Hike Bets Tempered by Iran War Economic Impact
item.description: European Central Bank policymakers are weighing inflation risks against growth hits from higher energy prices as traders reduce bets on multiple rate hikes this year.

item.title: Japan Intervenes to Prop Up Yen Against Dollar
item.description: Japan intervened in currency markets to support the embattled yen from near two-year lows against the dollar, marking a significant move amid global economic uncertainty.

item.title: EU to Remove Leather From Anti-Deforestation Law
item.description: The European Commission will exclude leather imports from its anti-deforestation regulation after industry pressure argued that production does not incentivize cattle farming driving forest destruction.

item.title: Ukraine Seeks Clarity from US on Russian Ceasefire Proposal
item.description: Ukraine will ask the Trump administration for clarification on a Russian proposal for a brief ceasefire, as diplomatic efforts continue amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Trump Criticizes German Chancellor Over Iran Interference
item.description: President Trump attacked Chancellor Friedrich Merz, telling him to focus on Germany problems and stop interfering with US efforts to address the Iran nuclear threat.

item.title: US Seeks International Coalition to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran threatens painful strikes if US renews attacks as Washington works to build a coalition to reopen the strategic waterway amid soaring crude prices.

item.title: EU-Mercosur Trade Pact Launches to Counter US Tariffs
item.description: The European Union and South American bloc Mercosur implement their free trade agreement, seeking to benefit exporters and offset the impact of US tariffs.

item.title: Oil Retreats After Hitting Four-Year High on Iran War Fears
item.description: Global oil prices eased after surging above 126 dollars per barrel, the highest in four years, as markets fear prolonged Middle East supply disruption.

item.title: US Combatant Commanders Request More Amphibious Ready Groups
item.description: All four-star officers leading U.S. military commands have requested amphibious ready groups and Marine expeditionary unit support, the Marine commandant told Defense News.

item.title: US Urges Lebanese President to Meet Netanyahu, Suggests IDF Pullout Possible
item.description: The U.S. embassy in Lebanon says direct engagement with Israel could lead to an IDF withdrawal, as political leaders weigh normalizing ties amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: US Naval Blockade Squeezes Iran Oil Exports, Forces Crude Onto Floating Storage
item.description: A U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports has shrunk Tehran's oil exports, stranding growing stockpiles of crude on tankers as buyers struggle to take delivery.

item.title: Ukraine imposes 2-month limit on front-line troops after emaciated soldiers spark outcry
item.description: Ukraine's top general ordered a mandatory two-month time limit for front-line troops serving in forward positions, responding to nationwide outrage after photos of emaciated soldiers on combat duty emerged.

item.title: Convicted Harvard scientist rebuilds brain-computer lab in China
item.description: An American scientist convicted of lying about payments from China has rebuilt his research lab in Shenzhen to pursue brain-computer interface technology that Beijing has identified as a national priority.

item.title: US military commanders to brief Trump on new Iran options as war deadline passes
item.description: Top US military leaders including the head of Central Command will brief President Trump on potential military action against Iran as a pivotal war deadline approaches with no clear end to the conflict in sight.

item.title: China, US economic chiefs hold candid call ahead of Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Top US and Chinese economic officials held frank talks ahead of a planned May meeting between Trump and Xi Jinping, with both sides raising complaints about the others trade policies amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: Bank of England holds rates, warns of Iran war inflation threat
item.description: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold and outlined potential economic impacts from the Iran war, warning that worst-case scenarios could force aggressive rate hikes while less damaging outcomes may require no increase.

item.title: Ukraine sees path to Japanese arms after Tokyo eases export rules
item.description: Japans relaxation of weapons-exports rules opens a path for talks that could lead to Tokyo supplying military equipment to help Ukraine resist Russias invasion, Kyivs ambassador to Japan told Reuters.

item.title: Palestinian football chief refuses photo with Israeli official at FIFA Congress
item.description: Jibril Rajoub declined to stand alongside Israeli representative Basim Sheikh Suliman at FIFA, saying "We are suffering." The Palestinian FA has appealed to CAS over West Bank settlement clubs.

item.title: Trump signs DHS funding bill ending partial shutdown, ICE and Border Patrol excluded
item.description: The bill restores funding for FEMA, Coast Guard, TSA and Secret Service after over two months. Democrats refused to fund immigration enforcement without restrictions on raids and mask-wearing by agents.

item.title: France investigates teenager in massive identity data breach affecting millions
item.description: A 15-year-old suspected of being hacker "breach3d" was detained for allegedly stealing 12-18 million data records from France's ID agency ANTS, including passport and ID card data, and attempting to sell them on the dark web.

item.title: Lebanon-Israel peace talks stall amid Saudi mediation efforts
item.description: Saudi attempts to broker peace talks between Lebanon and Israel have hit a roadblock due to internal Lebanese political divisions. The talks, which could reshape regional dynamics, remain deadlocked as Hezbollah's role remains contentious.

item.title: Trump voter roll push rebuffed by courts ahead of midterms
item.description: Federal courts have rejected Trump administration efforts to access state voter rolls, dealing a blow to election integrity initiatives ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. The rulings mark a significant setback for administration voting policy.

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill Nine in Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: At least nine people including two children were killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to Lebanese health ministry, in violation of the ceasefire agreement.
